post_topic: Layered systems thinking as a natural philosophy — how distributed system design mirrors how living things are built
image_description:
Single standalone diagram. One concept only. No panels, no grids, no collages. Full bleed 1080x1080px white background.

A vertical cross-section illustration oriented top-to-bottom showing six distinct horizontal strata layers, styled like a soil cross-section or geological cut with clean flat design. Each layer is a full-width rectangle with a label. From bottom to top: Layer 1 labeled 'Infrastructure' (deepest stratum, dark navy fill), Layer 2 labeled 'Data' (slightly lighter navy), Layer 3 labeled 'Compute' (medium blue), Layer 4 labeled 'Model' (blue-gray), Layer 5 labeled 'Agent Execution' (blue highlight), Layer 6 labeled 'UI' (topmost, lightest fill near white). Each layer has a small bracket on the right side with an amber callout annotation reading 'load-bearing' pointing upward with a thin arrow indicating the layer supports everything above it. A single vertical downward arrow on the left margin runs from UI at top to Infrastructure at the bottom, labeled in amber text: 'Build order matters'. The overall visual reads like a technical architecture cross-section diagram. No plant imagery, no photos — clean flat geometric shapes only.

Navy #0A0F1E primary elements, blue #5a96ff highlights, amber #F5A623 callouts and important labels. Clean sans-serif font. Real cloud provider logos where relevant. Small text "Kelvin Davis" bottom right corner. Professional LinkedIn infographic style similar to ByteByteGo. White background. No dark backgrounds.
